routed to the configured chat id\n329\t        # Body is produced by _format_telegram_message.\n330\t        assert \"Bead adc-1 done\" in sent[0][1]\n331\t\n332\t    async def test_returns_false_on_bridge_failure(self, store, router):\n333\t        \"\"\"A real call is made; the bridge's failure propagates, not a hard-coded value.\"\"\"\n334\t        fb, sent = self._fallback_with_chat_id(chat_id=1, send_return=False)\n335\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, telegram_fallback=fb)\n336\t\n337\t        ok = await w._send_to_telegram({\"summary\": \"hi\"}, \"session-1\")\n338\t\n339\t        assert ok is False\n340\t        assert len(sent) == 1  # the call was actually attempted\n341\t\n342\t    async def test_no_op_without_chat_id(self, store, router, caplog):\n343\t        \"\"\"No chat id configured → graceful WARNING + False, no send attempted.\"\"\"\n344\t        fb = TelegramFallback(bridge_url=\"http://test-bridge:8000\")\n345\t        assert fb.chat_id is None\n346\t        # Sentinel: if delivery were attempted, this would raise.\n347\t        async def boom(*a, **kw):\n348\t            raise AssertionError(\"send_message must not be called when chat_id is None\")\n349\t        fb.send_message = boom\n350\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, telegram_fallback=fb)\n351\t\n352\t        with caplog.at_level(\"WARNING\"):\n353\t            ok = await w._send_to_telegram({\"summary\": \"hi\"}, \"session-1\")\n354\t\n355\t        assert ok is False\n356\t        warnings = [r for r in caplog.records if r.levelname == \"WARNING\"]\n357\t        assert len(warnings) == 1\n358\t        assert \"ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID\" in warnings[0].message\n359\t\n360\t    async def test_uses_injected_fallback_not_singleton(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n361\t        \"\"\"The injected fallback wins over the module singleton.\"\"\"\n362\t        # Poison the singleton so any accidental use of it is caught.\n363\t        async def boom(*a, **kw):\n364\t            raise AssertionError(\"injected fallback must be used, not the singleton\")\n365\t        import src.watcher.daemon as daemon_mod\n366\t        singleton = TelegramFallback(chat_id=1)\n367\t        singleton.send_message = boom\n368\t        monkeypatch.setattr(daemon_mod, \"get_telegram_fallback\", lambda: singleton)\n369\t\n370\t        fb, sent = self._fallback_with_chat_id(chat_id=4242)\n371\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, telegram_fallback=fb)\n372\t\n373\t        ok = await w._send_to_telegram({\"summary\": \"hi\"}, \"session-1\")\n374\t\n375\t        assert ok is True\n376\t        assert sent[0][0] == 4242  # the injected fallback delivered\n377\t\n378\t\n379\t# --- lifespan supervision, liveness, interval (adc-4afi) -------------------\n380\t\n381\tclass _TaskCrash(BaseException):\n382\t    \"\"\"A task-killing crash.\n383\t\n384\t    Subclasses BaseException (not Exception) so it escapes the watch loop's\n385\t    transient ``except Exception`` catch -- simulating a real task death that\n386\t    only the supervisor layer can recover from. Normal exceptions raised from\n387\t    ``_check_for_events`` are caught by the loop and never reach the supervisor.\n388\t    \"\"\"\n389\t\n390\t\n391\tclass TestInterval:\n392\t    \"\"\"Poll interval defaults to 30s (plan §10) and is overridable.\n393\t\n394\t    Resolution order: explicit constructor arg > ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\n395\t    env > class default (30s).\n396\t    \"\"\"\n397\t\n398\t    def test_default_interval_is_30(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n399\t        # Guard against a stray env var in the test environment.\n400\t        monkeypatch.delenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", raising=False)\n401\t        assert BeadWatcher.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS == 30\n402\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n403\t        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 30.0\n404\t\n405\t    def test_constructor_arg_overrides_default(self, store, router):\n406\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=7)\n407\t        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 7.0\n408\t\n409\t    def test_env_overrides_default_when_no_arg(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n410\t        monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", \"12\")\n411\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n412\t        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 12.0\n413\t\n414\t    def test_explicit_arg_beats_env(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n415\t        monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", \"12\")\n416\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=9)\n417\t        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 9.0\n418\t\n419\t\n420\tclass TestLiveness:\n421\t    \"\"\"last_tick_at / tick_count advance on every tick under normal run.\"\"\"\n422\t\n423\t    def test_initial_liveness_state(self, store, router):\n424\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n425\t        assert w.last_tick_at == 0.0   # 0.0 == \"never ticked\" sentinel\n426\t        assert w.tick_count == 0\n427\t\n428\t    async def test_loop_stamps_last_tick_at_and_increments_tick_count(\n429\t        self, store, router, tmp_path, monkeypatch\n430\t    ):\n431\t        checkpoint = _write_checkpoint(tmp_path / \"issues.jsonl\", [])\n432\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, beads_jsonl=str(checkpoint), check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n433\t\n434\t        # Patch asyncio.sleep in the daemon module: the loop's interval sleep\n435\t        # (seconds < 1.0, since we set the interval to 0.0) becomes instant and\n436\t        # terminates the task after 3 completed ticks, so the test is\n437\t        # deterministic and never waits real time.\n438\t        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n439\t        interval_sleeps = {\"n\": 0}\n440\t\n441\t        async def fast_sleep(seconds):\n442\t            await real_sleep(0)  # yield to the scheduler\n443\t            if seconds < 1.0:\n444\t                interval_sleeps[\"n\"] += 1\n445\t                if interval_sleeps[\"n\"] >= 3:\n446\t                    raise asyncio.CancelledError()  # end the watch task\n447\t\n448\t        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", fast_sleep)\n449\t\n450\t        await w.start()\n451\t        for _ in range(200):\n452\t            if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n453\t                break\n454\t            await real_sleep(0.002)\n455\t        await w.stop()\n456\t\n457\t        # Each completed tick stamped liveness and bumped the counter.\n458\t        assert w.tick_count >= 3\n459\t        assert w.last_tick_at > 0.0\n460\t\n461\t\n462\tclass TestSupervisorRestart:\n463\t    \"\"\"A crashed watch task is restarted by the supervisor with growing backoff.\n464\t\n465\t    adc-4afi: the old bare create_task loop caught per-iteration exceptions but\n466\t    never restarted a task that ended -- once dead it stayed dead. The supervisor\n467\t    layer now respawns it on death with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, capped).\n468\t    \"\"\"\n469\t\n470\t    async def test_crashed_task_restarts_with_increasing_backoff(\n471\t        self, store, router, tmp_path, monkeypatch, caplog\n472\t    ):\n473\t        checkpoint = _write_checkpoint(tmp_path / \"issues.jsonl\", [])\n474\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, beads_jsonl=str(checkpoint), check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n475\t\n476\t        # Every tick crashes the task outright (escapes the transient catch).\n477\t        async def crash():\n478\t            raise _TaskCrash(\"forced crash\")\n479\t\n480\t        monkeypatch.setattr(w, \"_check_for_events\", crash)\n481\t\n482\t        # Capture the backoff durations the supervisor requests, without really\n483\t        # sleeping. After the 3rd backoff sleep, stop the supervisor so the test\n484\t        # terminates deterministically.\n485\t        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n486\t        requested = []\n487\t\n488\t        async def capture_sleep(seconds):\n489\t            requested.append(seconds)\n490\t            await real_sleep(0)\n491\t            if seconds >= 1.0 and sum(1 for s in requested if s >= 1.0) >= 3:\n492\t                raise asyncio.CancelledError()\n493\t\n494\t        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", capture_sleep)\n495\t\n496\t        with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger=\"src.watcher.daemon\"):\n497\t            await w.start()\n498\t            for _ in range(200):\n499\t                if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n500\t                    break\n501\t                await real_sleep(0.002)\n502\t            await w.stop()\n503\t\n504\t        backoffs = [s for s in requested if s >= 1.0]\n505\t        # Three crashes, each followed by a logged restart with growing backoff.\n506\t        assert len(backoffs) >= 3\n507\t        assert backoffs[0] == pytest.approx(2.0)\n508\t        assert backoffs[1] == pytest.approx(4.0)\n509\t        assert backoffs[2] == pytest.approx(8.0)\n510\t        assert w._restart_count >= 3\n511\t        restart_logs = [\n512\t            r for r in caplog.records if \"restarting\" in r.getMessage().lower()\n513\t        ]\n514\t        assert len(restart_logs) >= 3\n515\t\n516\t    async def test_transient_exception_does_not_trigger_restart(\n517\t        self, store, router, tmp_path, monkeypatch\n518\t    ):\n519\t        \"\"\"A normal Exception is caught by the loop -- no supervisor restart.\n520\t\n521\t        Pins the two-layer split: transient per-iteration errors stay absorbed\n522\t        by the watch loop (pre-supervisor behavior preserved); only a real task\n523\t        death reaches the supervisor. So no backoff is ever requested and the\n524\t        task keeps ticking despite the recurring error.\n525\t        \"\"\"\n526\t        checkpoint = _write_checkpoint(tmp_path / \"issues.jsonl\", [])\n527\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, beads_jsonl=str(checkpoint), check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n528\t\n529\t        async def transient():\n530\t            raise RuntimeError(\"transient per-iteration error\")\n531\t\n532\t        monkeypatch.setattr(w, \"_check_for_events\", transient)\n533\t\n534\t        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n535\t        sleeps = []\n536\t\n537\t        async def fast_sleep(seconds):\n538\t            sleeps.append(seconds)\n539\t            await real_sleep(0)\n540\t            if seconds < 1.0 and len([s for s in sleeps if s < 1.0]) >= 4:\n541\t                raise asyncio.CancelledError()\n542\t\n543\t        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", fast_sleep)\n544\t\n545\t        await w.start()\n546\t        for _ in range(200):\n547\t            if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n548\t                break\n549\t            await real_sleep(0.002)\n550\t        await w.stop()\n551\t\n552\t        # No backoff (>= 1.0) sleep was ever requested, and the task never died.\n553\t        assert not any(s >= 1.0 for s in sleeps)\n554\t        assert w._restart_count == 0\n555\t        # The loop kept ticking -- liveness advanced despite the errors.\n556\t        assert w.tick_count >= 4\n557\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7f87a610-ccee-4ae7-889d-e0f8bf76b488","uuid":"a54f5a24-fba2-4095-b49a-6cc1ab884d63","timestamp":"2026-07-22T12:45:01.090Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/tests/test_bead_watcher.py","content":"\"\"\"\nRegression tests for the bead watcher daemon (fixes for bead adc-5wtm).\n\nThe BeadWatcher was a complete silent no-op for two reasons:\n1. the injected fallback delivered\n\n\n# --- lifespan supervision, liveness, interval (adc-4afi) -------------------\n\nclass _TaskCrash(BaseException):\n    \"\"\"A task-killing crash.\n\n    Subclasses BaseException (not Exception) so it escapes the watch loop's\n    transient ``except Exception`` catch -- simulating a real task death that\n    only the supervisor layer can recover from. Normal exceptions raised from\n    ``_check_for_events`` are caught by the loop and never reach the supervisor.\n    \"\"\"\n\n\nclass TestInterval:\n    \"\"\"Poll interval defaults to 30s (plan §10) and is overridable.\n\n    Resolution order: explicit constructor arg > ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\n    env > class default (30s).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_default_interval_is_30(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n        # Guard against a stray env var in the test environment.\n        monkeypatch.delenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", raising=False)\n        assert BeadWatcher.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS == 30\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 30.0\n\n    def test_constructor_arg_overrides_default(self, store, router):\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=7)\n        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 7.0\n\n    def test_env_overrides_default_when_no_arg(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n        monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", \"12\")\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 12.0\n\n    def test_explicit_arg_beats_env(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n        monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", \"12\")\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=9)\n        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 9.0\n\n\nclass TestLiveness:\n    \"\"\"last_tick_at / tick_count advance on every tick under normal run.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_initial_liveness_state(self, store, router):\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n        assert w.last_tick_at == 0.0   # 0.0 == \"never ticked\" sentinel\n        assert w.tick_count == 0\n\n    async def test_loop_stamps_last_tick_at_and_increments_tick_count(\n        self, store, router, tmp_path, monkeypatch\n    ):\n        checkpoint = _write_checkpoint(tmp_path / \"issues.jsonl\", [])\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, beads_jsonl=str(checkpoint), check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n\n        # Patch asyncio.sleep in the daemon module: the loop's interval sleep\n        # (seconds < 1.0, since we set the interval to 0.0) becomes instant and\n        # terminates the task after 3 completed ticks, so the test is\n        # deterministic and never waits real time.\n        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n        interval_sleeps = {\"n\": 0}\n\n        async def fast_sleep(seconds):\n            await real_sleep(0)  # yield to the scheduler\n            if seconds < 1.0:\n                interval_sleeps[\"n\"] += 1\n                if interval_sleeps[\"n\"] >= 3:\n                    raise asyncio.CancelledError()  # end the watch task\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", fast_sleep)\n\n        await w.start()\n        for _ in range(200):\n            if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n                break\n            await real_sleep(0.002)\n        await w.stop()\n\n        # Each completed tick stamped liveness and bumped the counter.\n        assert w.tick_count >= 3\n        assert w.last_tick_at > 0.0\n\n\nclass TestSupervisorRestart:\n    \"\"\"A crashed watch task is restarted by the supervisor with growing backoff.\n\n    adc-4afi: the old bare create_task loop caught per-iteration exceptions but\n    never restarted a task that ended -- once dead it stayed dead. The supervisor\n    layer now respawns it on death with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, capped).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    async def test_crashed_task_restarts_with_increasing_backoff(\n        self, store, router, tmp_path, monkeypatch, caplog\n    ):\n        checkpoint = _write_checkpoint(tmp_path / \"issues.jsonl\", [])\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, beads_jsonl=str(checkpoint), check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n\n        # Every tick crashes the task outright (escapes the transient catch).\n        async def crash():\n            raise _TaskCrash(\"forced crash\")\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(w, \"_check_for_events\", crash)\n\n        # Capture the backoff durations the supervisor requests, without really\n        # sleeping. After the 3rd backoff sleep, stop the supervisor so the test\n        # terminates deterministically.\n        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n        requested = []\n\n        async def capture_sleep(seconds):\n            requested.append(seconds)\n            await real_sleep(0)\n            if seconds >= 1.0 and sum(1 for s in requested if s >= 1.0) >= 3:\n                raise asyncio.CancelledError()\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", capture_sleep)\n\n        with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger=\"src.watcher.daemon\"):\n            await w.start()\n            for _ in range(200):\n                if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n                    break\n                await real_sleep(0.002)\n            await w.stop()\n\n        backoffs = [s for s in requested if s >= 1.0]\n        # Three crashes, each followed by a logged restart with growing backoff.\n        assert len(backoffs) >= 3\n        assert backoffs[0] == pytest.approx(2.0)\n        assert backoffs[1] == pytest.approx(4.0)\n        assert backoffs[2] == pytest.approx(8.0)\n        assert w._restart_count >= 3\n        restart_logs = [\n            r for r in caplog.records if \"restarting\" in r.getMessage().lower()\n        ]\n        assert len(restart_logs) >= 3\n\n    async def test_transient_exception_does_not_trigger_restart(\n        self, store, router, tmp_path, monkeypatch\n    ):\n        \"\"\"A normal Exception is caught by the loop -- no supervisor restart.\n\n        Pins the two-layer split: transient per-iteration errors stay absorbed\n        by the watch loop (pre-supervisor behavior preserved); only a real task\n        death reaches the supervisor. So no backoff is ever requested and the\n        task keeps ticking despite the recurring error.\n        \"\"\"\n        checkpoint = _write_checkpoint(tmp_path / \"issues.jsonl\", [])\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, beads_jsonl=str(checkpoint), check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n\n        async def transient():\n            raise RuntimeError(\"transient per-iteration error\")\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(w, \"_check_for_events\", transient)\n\n        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n        sleeps = []\n\n        async def fast_sleep(seconds):\n            sleeps.append(seconds)\n            await real_sleep(0)\n            if seconds < 1.0 and len([s for s in sleeps if s < 1.0]) >= 4:\n                raise asyncio.CancelledError()\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", fast_sleep)\n\n        await w.start()\n        for _ in range(200):\n            if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n                break\n            await real_sleep(0.002)\n        await w.stop()\n\n        # No backoff (>= 1.0) sleep was ever requested, and the task never died.\n        assert not any(s >= 1.0 for s in sleeps)\n        assert w._restart_count == 0\n        # The loop kept ticking -- liveness advanced despite the errors.\n        assert w.tick_count >= 4\n","numLines":557,"startLine":1,"totalLines":557}}}

Optional ``since`` (unix timestamp) windows to recent\n+    dispatches only.\n+    \"\"\"\n+    store = await get_store()\n+    return await store.get_latency_percentiles(since=since)\n+\n+\n @app.post(\"/escalate\")\n async def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n     \"\"\"\n@@ -670,6 +742,31 @@ async def get_environment():\n     }\n \n \n+@app.get(\"/api/v1/registry\")\n+async def get_registry_endpoint():\n+    \"\"\"Return the merged project registry (config/registry.yaml + discovery).\n+\n+    This is the DB-independent data source for the first-run welcome card (the","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

This layer handles the case the\n+        inner loop cannot: the task ending outright (an exception that is not a\n+        transient ``Exception`` -- e.g. a ``BaseException`` raised mid-tick --\n+        or the loop returning). On such a death, while we are still meant to be\n+        running, we log and respawn the task after an exponentially-growing\n+        backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, ... capped at 32s). Backoff resets to the short\n+        end once a tick completes successfully (see ``_watch_loop``).\n+\n+        ``stop()`` sets ``_running = False`` before cancelling the supervisor,\n+        so a clean shutdown is distinguished from an unexpected task death by\n+        the ``_running`` flag rather than by exception type.\n+        \"\"\"\n+        while self._running:\n+            self._watch_task = asyncio.create_task(\n+                self._watch_loop(),\n+                name=f\"bead-watcher-tick-{self._restart_count}\",\n+            )\n+            crash_reason: Optional[BaseException] = None\n+            try:\n+                await self._watch_task\n+            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n+                # Supervisor itself cancelled -- only stop() does that. Exit.\n+                return\n+            except (KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit):\n+                # Process-level signals: never swallow, let them propagate.\n+                raise\n+            except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — must catch task death\n+                crash_reason = exc\n+\n+            if not self._running:\n+                # stop() raced with the task ending -- clean shutdown.\n+                return\n+\n+            self._restart_count += 1\n+            backoff = self._next_backoff()\n+            if crash_reason is not None:\n+                logger.warning(\n+                    \"Bead watcher task crashed (%s); restarting in %.1fs \"\n+                    \"(restart #%d)\",\n+                    type(crash_reason).__name__,\n+                    backoff,\n+                    self._restart_count,\n+                    exc_info=True,\n+                )\n+            else:\n+                logger.warning(\n+                    \"Bead watcher task ended unexpectedly; restarting in %.1fs \"\n+                    \"(restart #%d)\",\n+                    backoff,\n+                    self._restart_count,\n+                )\n+            try:\n+                await asyncio.sleep(backoff)\n+            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n+                return\n+\n+    def _next_backoff(self) -> float:\n+        \"\"\"Backoff (seconds) for the upcoming restart, growing with crashes.\n+\n+        ``_restart_count`` is incremented to >= 1 before each call, so the\n+        first restart waits RESTART_BACKOFF_SECONDS[0] (2s), the next the\n+        following entry (4s), and so on; once the count exceeds the schedule\n+        the final (cap) value is reused.\n+        \"\"\"\n+        idx = min(self._restart_count - 1, len(self.RESTART_BACKOFF_SECONDS) - 1)\n+        return self.RESTART_BACKOFF_SECONDS[idx]\n+\n+    def _stamp_tick(self) -> None:\n+        \"\"\"Record liveness after a completed poll tick.\n+\n+        Called after every tick body resolves (success or a caught transient\n+        error). GET /health's watcher block reads these: ``last_tick_at``\n+        within 2x the interval means the task is alive, and ``tick_count`` is\n+        the cumulative tick count. (adc-4afi, consumed by child 3.)\n+        \"\"\"\n+        self.last_tick_at = time.time()\n+        self.tick_count += 1\n+\n     async def _watch_loop(self) -> None:\n-        \"\"\"Main watch loop - checks for new bead events.\"\"\"\n+        \"\"\"Main watch loop - checks for new bead events each interval.\n+\n+        Each iteration runs the detection tick (``_check_for_events``), stamps\n+        liveness, then sleeps for the poll interval. This layer handles the case the\n+        inner loop cannot: the task ending outright (an exception that is not a\n+        transient ``Exception`` -- e.g. a ``BaseException`` raised mid-tick --\n+        or the loop returning). On such a death, while we are still meant to be\n+        running, we log and respawn the task after an exponentially-growing\n+        backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, ... capped at 32s). Backoff resets to the short\n+        end once a tick completes successfully (see ``_watch_loop``).\n+\n+        ``stop()`` sets ``_running = False`` before cancelling the supervisor,\n+        so a clean shutdown is distinguished from an unexpected task death by\n+        the ``_running`` flag rather than by exception type.\n+        \"\"\"\n+        while self._running:\n+            self._watch_task = asyncio.create_task(\n+                self._watch_loop(),\n+                name=f\"bead-watcher-tick-{self._restart_count}\",\n+            )\n+            crash_reason: Optional[BaseException] = None\n+            try:\n+                await self._watch_task\n+            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n+                # Supervisor itself cancelled -- only stop() does that. Exit.\n+                return\n+            except (KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit):\n+                # Process-level signals: never swallow, let them propagate.\n+                raise\n+            except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — must catch task death\n+                crash_reason = exc\n+\n+            if not self._running:\n+                # stop() raced with the task ending -- clean shutdown.\n+                return\n+\n+            self._restart_count += 1\n+            backoff = self._next_backoff()\n+            if crash_reason is not None:\n+                logger.warning(\n+                    \"Bead watcher task crashed (%s); Per-iteration exceptions\n+        are caught and logged so a transient error never ends the task -- the\n+        supervisor layer restarts the task only when it truly dies.
